'''Saclas''' was a barren [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]] [[Planet/Legends|planet]] located in the [[Corporate Sector/Legends|Corporate Sector]]. [[Homeworld/Legends|Home]] to [[gelatinous pet slime]]s, it was a [[Colonist/Legends|colony]] world of the [[Sentience/Legends|sentient]] [[Ocsin]] [[Species/Legends|species]]. Saclas was frequently visited both by Ocsin [[Merchant/Legends|merchants]] travelling to [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]]–[[Imperial Space|controlled space]] and [[free-trader]]s and [[Smuggler/Legends|smugglers]] making cautious trips into [[Corporate Sector Authority/Legends|Corporate Sector Authority]] territory, and the world had a rough-and-tumble reputation. The Ocsin future member of the [[Zulirian Swordmasters]] group [[Cene Gilvent]] grew up on Saclas, where she eventually earned enough [[Currency/Legends|money]] to travel offworld.

==Description==
Saclas was a barren [[Planet/Legends|planet]]<ref name="AIR" /> that was situated in the [[Saclas system/Legends|Saclas system]]<ref name="EAOC" /> and was located just inside the borders of<ref name="TEA" /> the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories']] [[Corporate Sector/Legends|Corporate Sector]].<ref name="EAOC" /> It was situated on the [[Solenbaran Merchant Route]] [[Hyperspace route/Legends|hyperlane]], which linked it to the [[Jerrist system/Legends|Jerrist system]] as well as the neighboring [[Wyl sector/Legends|Wyl sector]]. Saclas also lay at one endpoint of the route known as the [[Tertiary Solenbaran]], which connected it to the [[Duroon system/Legends|Duroon system]].<ref name="TEA" /> The planet was of little overall importance but was renowned for its [[gelatinous pet slime]]s, which were highly popular among young members of the [[Sentience/Legends|sentient]] [[Gamorrean/Legends|Gamorrean]] [[Species/Legends|species]],<ref name="AIR" /> [[Homeworld/Legends|native]] to the planet [[Gamorr/Legends|Gamorr]]<ref name="TEA" /> in the Outer Rim's [[Galov sector/Legends|Galov sector]].<ref name="EAOC Opoku">{{SW|url=news/star-wars-the-essential-atlas-online-companion|text=''Star Wars: The Essential Atlas'' Online Companion|int=Star Wars: The Essential Atlas Online Companion}} — Based on corresponding data for [[Gamorr system/Legends|Opoku system]]</ref>

==History==
Saclas was under the control of the [[Corporate Sector Authority/Legends|Corporate Sector Authority]], the entity administrating the Corporate Sector.<ref name="TEA" /> The [[Gender/Legends|female]] [[Ocsin]] [[Cene Gilvent]] grew up on Saclas, where, during her late teenage [[Standard year/Legends|years]], she earned enough [[Currency/Legends|money]] harvesting [[gelatin]]ous pet [[Slime/Legends|slimes]] to buy passage to the [[Mid Rim/Legends|Mid Rim]], where she eventually became a member of the [[Zulirian Swordmasters]] group of [[Melee weapon/Legends|melee weapon]] afficionados.<ref name="AIR" />

==Inhabitants==
Saclas was a [[Colonist/Legends|colony]] world of the Ocsin species, which hailed from the [[Wild Space/Legends|Wild Space]] celestial body [[Farana/Legends|Farana]]. It was frequently visited by Ocsin [[Merchant/Legends|merchants]] who were traveling down the Solenbaran Merchant Route to [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]]–[[Imperial Space|controlled space]]. Another group of common visitors to Saclas were [[free-trader]]s and [[Smuggler/Legends|smugglers]] who avoided entering deeper into the Corporate Sector Authority's territory.<ref name="TEA" />

==Locations==
The rough-and-tumble<ref name="EotECR">[[Star Wars: Edge of the Empire Core Rulebook|''Star Wars: Edge of the Empire'' Core Rulebook]]</ref> Saclas featured an assortment of hardscrabble [[Spaceport/Legends|spaceports]] and [[Cantina/Legends|cantinas]] which likewise had a reputation of being rough.<ref name="TEA" />

==Behind the scenes==
Saclas was first mentioned in ''[[Alliance Intelligence Reports]]'', a [[1995]] sourcebook for [[West End Games]]' ''[[Star Wars: The Roleplaying Game]]''.<ref name="AIR" /> The [[2009]] reference book ''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' subsequently placed it in grid square S-4.<ref name="TEA" />
